# ATI CMS Maternal & Newborn — Cheat Sheet
### Comprehensive Quick Reference (All OB Chapters)

---

## 1. FETAL DEVELOPMENT (Ch. 3)

### Fertilization & Early Development
- Fertilization occurs in **outer third of fallopian tube**
- Sperm viable **5 days**, ovum viable **24 hours**
- Zygote → Morula (day 3) → Blastocyst → Implants (~day 7)
- Implants in **upper posterior uterine wall**
- Decidua basalis = maternal portion of placenta

### Germ Layers
| Layer | Structures |
|-------|-----------|
| **Ectoderm** | Skin, hair, nails, nervous system, sense organs |
| **Mesoderm** | Skeleton, muscles, blood vessels, kidneys, gonads |
| **Endoderm** | GI lining, trachea, bronchi, bladder, urethra |

### Milestones
- **8 weeks**: All systems formed → enters fetal period
- **20 weeks**: Age of viability (textbook)
- **37 weeks**: Term; **39-40 weeks**: Full term
- Amniotic fluid: 30mL (10wk) → 350mL (20wk) → **1000mL (37wk)**

### Placental Hormones
| Hormone | Function |
|---------|----------|
| **Progesterone** | Maintains lining, prevents contractions, preps breasts |
| **Estrogen** | Uterine growth, blood flow, breast duct development |
| **hCG** | Signals conception, maintains corpus luteum (detected by pregnancy tests) |
| **hPL** | Decreases maternal insulin sensitivity → more glucose to fetus |

### Umbilical Cord: **2 arteries** (waste away) + **1 vein** (O2 to fetus) + Wharton jelly

### Three Fetal Shunts ⭐
| Shunt | Bypasses | Closes |
|-------|---------|--------|
| **Ductus venosus** | Liver | Cord cut (perm ~1 wk) |
| **Foramen ovale** | Lungs (R→L atrium) | **2 hrs** (perm ~3 mo) |
| **Ductus arteriosus** | Lungs (PA→aorta) | **15 hrs** (perm ~3 wk) |

### Twins
- **Monozygotic** = identical, 1 egg, shared chorion/placenta
- **Dizygotic** = fraternal, 2 eggs, separate placentas

---

## 2. PRENATAL CARE & ADAPTATIONS (Ch. 4)

### OB Terminology
- **GTPAL**: Gravida, Term, Preterm, Abortions, Living
- **Para** = births ≥20 weeks (alive or still)

### EDD: Nagele's Rule ⭐
> **LMP − 3 months + 7 days** (average pregnancy = 40 weeks/280 days)

### Signs of Pregnancy
| Presumptive | Probable | Positive |
|------------|---------|---------|
| Amenorrhea, N/V, fatigue | Goodell (cervix soft) | FHR heard by examiner |
| Breast changes, quickening | Chadwick (purplish cervix) | Fetal movement felt by examiner |
| Urinary frequency | Hegar (lower uterus soft) | Fetus on ultrasound |
| Deepening pigmentation | +hCG test, Braxton Hicks | |

### Prenatal Labs by Trimester
- **1st**: Blood type/Rh, CBC, RPR, rubella, TB, HepB, HIV, UA, Pap, STI screen, NIPT
- **2nd**: Quad screen, anatomy US, **GDM screen (24-28 wk)**
- **3rd**: **GBS culture (35-37 wk)**, NST/BPP, fetal fibronectin

### Four Key Nutrients
| Nutrient | Amount | Why |
|----------|--------|-----|
| Protein | 60 g/day | Tissue growth/repair |
| Calcium | 1000 mg | Fetal skeleton (needs Vit D) |
| Iron | 30 mg/day | Prevents anemia |
| Folic acid | 400-800 mcg | Prevents **neural tube defects** |

> ⚠️ Excess Vitamin A = teratogenic | B6 + ginger = for nausea | PICA = eating non-food items

### Supine Hypotension → **Turn to LEFT SIDE** (IVC compression)

### Weight gain: **25-35 lbs** (normal BMI)

---

## 3. PREGNANCY COMPLICATIONS (Ch. 5)

### Danger Signs — REPORT IMMEDIATELY
Sudden fluid gush, vaginal bleeding, abdominal pain, persistent vomiting, epigastric pain, face/hand edema, severe headache, blurred vision, fever >100.4°F, dysuria

### Placenta Previa vs. Abruption ⭐
| | Previa | Abruption |
|--|--------|-----------|
| Bleeding | **Painless**, bright red | **Painful**, dark |
| Uterus | Soft | Firm, board-like |
| Risk factors | Prior C-sections | Cocaine, HTN |
| Key point | **No vaginal exams** | Monitor for DIC |

### Preeclampsia ⭐
- **Triad**: HTN + Proteinuria + Edema (after 20 wk)
- **HELLP**: Hemolysis, Elevated Liver enzymes, Low Platelets
- Tx: **Magnesium sulfate** (seizures), labetalol (BP)
- **Cure = DELIVERY**

### GDM
- Screen **24-28 weeks**; risks: macrosomia, neonatal hypoglycemia
- True GDM → glucose normalizes by **6 weeks postpartum**

### Rh Incompatibility
- Rh− mom + Rh+ baby → **erythroblastosis fetalis**
- **RhoGAM** at 28 wk + within 72 hrs after delivery

### TORCH Infections
**T**oxoplasmosis (cat feces) | **O**ther (syphilis, GBS, HepB) | **R**ubella (vaccine before pregnancy) | **C**MV | **H**erpes (C-section if active lesions)

---

## 4. LABOR & BIRTH (Ch. 6)

### Four Ps: **Powers, Passage, Passenger, Psyche**

### Signs of Labor
Bloody show, ROM, regular contractions, lightening, nesting, weight loss

### True vs. False Labor
- **True**: Regular, increasing intensity, cervical change, not stopped by position change
- **False**: Irregular, no cervical change, stops with rest/position change

### Cardinal Movements ⭐
**Descent → Engagement → Flexion → Internal Rotation → Extension → External Rotation → Expulsion**

### Stages of Labor
| Stage | What Happens | Duration |
|-------|-------------|----------|
| 1st | Dilation & effacement (0-10 cm) | 4-6 hrs |
| 2nd | Expulsion of fetus | 30 min - 2 hrs |
| 3rd | Placenta delivery | 5-30 min |
| 4th | Recovery | 1-2 hrs |

### FHR Monitoring ⭐
- Normal baseline: **110-160 bpm**
- VEAL CHOP: **V**ariable = **C**ord compression | **E**arly = **H**ead compression | **A**ccelerations = **O**K | **L**ate = **P**lacental insufficiency
- Reassuring: moderate variability, accelerations
- Non-reassuring: late decels, variable decels, absent variability

### Amniotic Fluid
- Normal: clear, flecks of vernix, no odor
- **Green** = meconium (fetal distress) | **Foul odor** = infection

### Apgar Score (1 & 5 min): **H**eart rate, **R**espiratory effort, **M**uscle tone, **R**eflex response, **C**olor (7-10 normal)

### Newborn Meds: **Erythromycin eye ointment** + **Vitamin K (AquaMEPHYTON)**

### Placenta Delivery: "Shiny Schulze" (fetal side) vs "Dirty Duncan" (maternal side)

---

## 5. PAIN MANAGEMENT IN LABOR (Ch. 7)

### Nonpharmacologic
- **Methods**: Dick-Read, Bradley, Lamaze
- **Techniques**: Effleurage, breathing, relaxation, counterpressure
- **Advantages**: No harm to mother/fetus, no drug side effects, won't slow labor
- **Gate control theory**: Touch/massage blocks pain signals

### Hyperventilation Signs ⭐
Dizziness, tingling hands/feet, numbness around nose/mouth, hand cramps
> **Fix**: Slow breathing, breathe into cupped hands, moist washcloth, brief breath-hold

### Pharmacologic
- **Analgesic** = blocks pain | **Anesthetic** = blocks pain + motor
- **Opioids**: Avoid within **1 hour** of birth (neonatal respiratory depression) → antidote: **naloxone**
- Two patients medicated: **mother AND fetus**

### Types of Anesthesia
| Type | Use |
|------|-----|
| Local infiltration | Episiotomy/laceration repair |
| Pudendal block | Perineal area |
| **Epidural** | Most common for labor |
| Spinal (subarachnoid) | Cesarean birth |
| General | Emergency C-section only |

- General anesthesia risk: **aspiration of gastric contents**
- Pregnancy increases aspiration risk (sluggish GI + aortocaval compression)

---

## 6. LABOR COMPLICATIONS (Ch. 8)

### Perineal Lacerations
- 1st degree: superficial mucosa/skin
- 2nd degree: deeper perineal tissues
- 3rd degree: + anal sphincter
- 4th degree: extends through rectal mucosa

### Cesarean Birth Incisions
- **Low transverse**: VBAC possible, least rupture risk
- **Classic (vertical)**: highest rupture risk, rarely used

### Obstetric Emergencies
- **Prolapsed cord**: Knee-chest position, hand in to lift presenting part off cord, emergency C-section
- **Amniotic fluid embolism**: Respiratory support, treat shock, replace coagulation factors → ICU
- **Uterine rupture**: Emergency surgical intervention

### Preterm Labor
- Risk factors: prior preterm birth, infection, multiples, PROM, substance abuse
- **Fetal fibronectin** test predicts preterm labor risk
- Treatment: **tocolytics** (terbutaline, nifedipine, MgSO4) + **betamethasone** (lung maturity, give 48 hrs before delivery)
- Tocolytic contraindications: preeclampsia, placenta previa, abruption, >37 wk, fetal demise

### Bishop Score: ≥6 predicts successful labor induction

---

## 7. POSTPARTUM CARE (Ch. 9)

### BUBBLE Assessment ⭐
- **B**reasts (soft vs engorged)
- **U**terus (firmness, fundal height, location)
- **B**ladder (distention)
- **B**owels (gas, bowel sounds, stool)
- **L**ochia (character, amount, color, odor)
- **E**pisiotomy/perineum (REEDA: Redness, Edema, Ecchymosis, Discharge, Approximation)

### Lochia Progression ⭐
| Type | Color | Duration |
|------|-------|----------|
| **Rubra** | Red (mostly blood) | Days 1-3 |
| **Serosa** | Pink (blood + mucus) | Days 4-10 |
| **Alba** | White/clear (mucus) | Days 10-21 |

### Involution
- Uterus descends **1-2 cm/day**; not palpable by **day 10**
- Returns to pre-pregnant size by **5-6 weeks**
- Boggy fundus = **uterine atony** → massage fundus

### Perineum Care
- Ice pack first **12-24 hours** → Sitz bath after 24 hours
- REEDA assessment for healing

### Breastfeeding
- **Prolactin** = milk production | **Oxytocin** = let-down reflex
- Colostrum (first days) → Transitional milk (7-10 days) → Mature milk (14 days)
- **Foremilk** (first) vs **Hindmilk** (higher fat, satisfies hunger)
- Feed every **2-3 hours** → 8-10 wet diapers/day
- Engorgement around **day 3** — keep feeding frequently
- Contraindications: **HIV, HTLV 1 & 2**
- Storage: room temp 3 hrs, fridge 3 days, freezer 3 months

### Rubin's Phases: Taking-in (dependent) → Taking-hold (independent) → Letting-go (role adjustment)

### Car Seat: Rear-facing in back seat until **age 2**, harness snug

---

## 8. POSTPARTUM COMPLICATIONS (Ch. 10)

### Postpartum Hemorrhage ⭐
- Early: within **24 hours** | Late: 24 hrs to **6 weeks**
- Defined as >500 mL (vaginal) or >1000 mL (C-section)
- **4 T's**: Tone (atony), Trauma, Tissue (retained placenta), Thrombin (clotting)
- **#1 cause: Uterine atony** → boggy fundus, heavy lochia
- Tachycardia is **earliest sign** of hypovolemic shock
- Tx: Fundal massage, empty bladder, oxytocin, methylergonovine (Methergine)

### Subinvolution
- Slower than expected uterine return to normal
- Signs: fundal height > expected, persistent lochia rubra, pelvic pain
- Tx: Methergine, antibiotics, D&C if needed

### Thromboembolic Disorders
- **SVT** → heat, elevation, analgesics
- **DVT** → heparin/LMWH (Lovenox), leg elevation; confirm by ultrasound
- **PE** → sudden chest pain, dyspnea, cough → ICU
- Prevention: early ambulation, SCDs, avoid popliteal pressure

### Infections
- **Puerperal sepsis**: Fever ≥100.4°F within first 10 days PP
- **Endometritis**: Most common cause of PP fever
- **Mastitis**: Breast infection — continue breastfeeding, antibiotics, warm compresses before feeding, ice after
- Weaning during mastitis **worsens** symptoms

### Mood Disorders
| Type | Onset | Features |
|------|-------|---------|
| **Baby blues** | Days 1-10 | Tearfulness, mood swings, resolves spontaneously |
| **PP Depression** | 2-4 weeks | Persistent sadness, inability to cope, guilt, sleep/appetite changes |
| **PP Psychosis** | Variable | Impaired reality, hallucinations — psychiatric emergency |

> Never assume "baby blues" — explore persistent feelings

---

## 9. THE TERM NEWBORN (Ch. 12)

### Head Assessment
- Normal circumference: **32-36 cm** (no more than 2 cm > chest)
- **Fontanelles**: Flat = normal | Bulging = increased ICP | Depressed = dehydration
- **Molding**: Overlapping skull bones (normal, resolves)
- **Caput succedaneum**: Soft tissue swelling (crosses suture lines)
- **Cephalohematoma**: Bleeding under periosteum (does NOT cross suture lines)

### Vital Signs
- Temp: **36.2-37.7°C** (97.1-99.8°F)
- HR: 110-160 bpm | RR: 30-60

### Thermoregulation — Four Heat Loss Mechanisms
| Type | How | Prevention |
|------|-----|-----------|
| **Evaporation** | Wet skin | Dry immediately after birth |
| **Conduction** | Cold surfaces | Warm blankets, skin-to-skin |
| **Convection** | Air currents | Avoid drafts, keep warm room |
| **Radiation** | Cold nearby objects | Keep away from cold walls/windows |

### Newborn Reflexes
- **Rooting**: Touch cheek → turns toward stimulus
- **Sucking**: Object in mouth → sucks
- **Moro (startle)**: Sudden noise → arms extend then flex
- **Grasp**: Object in palm → grips

### Stool Progression
**Meconium** (dark green/black, sterile) → **Transitional** → **Breast-fed** (yellow, seedy) or **Formula-fed** (pale yellow, firmer)

### Neonatal Hypoglycemia
- Blood glucose < **45 mg/dL**
- Risk: preterm, LGA/SGA, diabetic mother, cold stress
- Signs: jitteriness, poor tone, poor suck, high-pitched cry, lethargy, seizures

### Cord Care: Clean and dry, diaper below cord, report redness/odor, sponge bath until stump falls off

### Infection Prevention: **Hand hygiene** is #1; immature immune system = slow response

### Newborn Screening: Hearing screen before discharge, metabolic screening

---

*⭐ = Highest-yield topics for ATI CMS. Focus on the shunts, Nagele's rule, BUBBLE assessment, lochia stages, FHR patterns (VEAL CHOP), preeclampsia management, postpartum hemorrhage, and newborn thermoregulation.*
